title: Contributing to these docs
description: How to add factory documentation pages to the you-agent-factory docs site without recreating retired product patterns.
openingSummary: Use this page when you want to add you-agent-factory documentation. It orients contributors to the live collections, production templates, isolation-first writing, and ordinary local validation path without pasting the full maintainer runbook.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This docs site documents the you-agent-factory CLI and agent-factory workflow system. Readers use it to install and run the CLI, follow use-case guides, look up concepts and techniques, read documentation topics, browse the glossary, and follow blog posts. It is not Model Atlas, not a benchmark leaderboard, and not a paper-download mirror. Contributing here means adding factory docs for those live surfaces, not recreating retired Atlas collections or promising future Model Atlas content.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: Choose a collection that matches the reader job: guides for step-by-step paths, concepts for named ideas, techniques for named workflow patterns, documentation for reference topics, glossary for short definitions, or blog for time-specific narrative posts. Each canonical docs page keeps structure in MDX, reader-facing prose in colocated message files, and discovery metadata in a registry record. Production templates under docs/templates supply the starter MDX, messages, and assets shape. Isolation-first writing means the page teaches its topic on its own without page-meta framing or reader-shortcut callouts.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Pick the matching collection, copy the production template and starter message and asset sidecars into a new page bundle, fill isolation-first prose in messages, and add the matching registry record. Run make validate-data as the ordinary local validation path before opening a pull request, then follow any related contributor checks the repository documents for content work. Deeper maintainer references stay in the repository: site fundamentals, the documentation template, writing standards, and the full CONTRIBUTING guide. Use the links below for those references and for nearby published factory pages.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is published contributing orientation for factory docs. It is not a paste of every validation command, not a second full CONTRIBUTING runbook, and not a place to recreate retired Atlas collection ownership. Product framing for the CLI itself belongs on what is you-agent-factory; first-run steps belong on getting started.
